subject: DIRECTIVE: GWT broadcast → trigger_activations telemetry — fleet-wide rollout spec

content.directive: Implement GWT broadcast → trigger_activations recording in all mesh agents
content.rationale: Session 94 Level 3 diagnostic found trigger_activations table (schema v23) contained 0 rows across the fleet. In-context trigger evaluation — the majority of cogarch governance — produced no observable telemetry. The basal ganglia reinforcement loop, tier promotion/demotion analysis, and DMN consolidation cron all depend on this data. Without it, the system cannot learn which checks catch and which waste attention.

mechanism.summary: gwt-broadcast.sh PostToolUse hook now parses GWT broadcast lines and records each to trigger_activations in state.db. The agent writes broadcasts during CRITICAL check execution (existing convention). The hook reads them and closes the loop to the database.
mechanism.result_values: pass | fail (optional, defaults to pass if omitted)
mechanism.deduplication: Per-cycle recorded-lines tracker (/tmp/{AGENT_ID}-gwt-recorded) prevents double-counting within a response cycle. Clears when broadcast file clears (5-minute staleness).
mechanism.session_id_source: session-start-orient.sh writes session ID to /tmp/{AGENT_ID}-session-id at session start. gwt-broadcast.sh reads it. Defaults to 0 if missing.

files_changed.change: Added broadcast line parser, trigger_activations recording via dual_write.py, dedup tracker. Uses AGENT_ID from _debug.sh for multi-agent compatibility.
Added session ID persistence to /tmp/{AGENT_ID}-session-id after T1 recording.
Updated GWT broadcast format (added result tag), documented telemetry closure.

fleet_rollout_requirements: Each agent needs dual_write.py trigger-activation subcommand (already in psychology-agent schema v23 — verify schema parity across agents)
Each agent needs trigger_activations table in state.db (schema v23 — run bootstrap if missing)
Each agent needs _debug.sh shared hook helper with AGENT_ID resolution
session-start-orient.sh must write session ID to /tmp/{AGENT_ID}-session-id
gwt-broadcast.sh must receive the updated version with the recording loop
Agents must adopt the enriched broadcast format: [BROADCAST T{n}#{check} {result}] {finding}
content.structural_limitation: The agent decides whether to write a broadcast. Silent governance bypass (agent skips a CRITICAL check entirely) produces no broadcast and no activation record. This circularity cannot resolve within the agent itself — only Tier 2/3 evaluator transcript review can detect silent bypass. The telemetry observes what the agent reports, not what the agent does.
content.validation: Tested end-to-end on psychology-agent Session 94. Two test broadcasts (T3#5 fail, T2#1 pass) recorded correctly to trigger_activations with session_id, trigger_id, check_number, tier, result, and action_taken.

action_gate.gate_condition: Operations-agent deploys updated hooks across fleet

epistemic_flags: Observability gap remains for silent governance bypass — agent self-reporting cannot catch what the agent chose not to report
trigger_activations data requires 10+ sessions before catch-rate analysis produces meaningful statistics

setl.S: H — fundamental observability gap in cogarch governance closed
setl.E: S — 3 files changed, convention enrichment + hook enhancement
setl.T: S — tested on psychology-agent, fleet rollout required
setl.L: M — structural limitation (self-reporting circularity) documented but not resolved
